title: Fermented Jamaican Pilaf
description: A deliciously fermented and tender Jamaican dish made with dark brown soft sugar, tempering-chocolate, and served in scallop shells. This dish is perfect for a romantic dinner.

ingredients.name: Dark brown soft sugar
ingredients.notes: Use high-quality, unrefined sugar for best flavor.

ingredients.name: Coconut milk
ingredients.notes: Full-fat coconut milk for best flavor.

ingredients.name: Spices
ingredients.notes: Cinnamon, nutmeg, and a pinch of salt.

ingredients.name: Whey
ingredients.notes: Fresh whey from yogurt or cheese.

instructions: Combine rice, coconut milk, water, and spices in a saucepan. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer for 15 minutes.
In a separate bowl, mix together whey, fermented vinegar, and tempering-chocolate. Whisk until smooth and set aside.
After the rice has finished cooking, fluff it with a fork and stir in the tempering-chocolate mixture.
Transfer the rice mixture to the scallop shells, then sprinkle with herbs and serve immediately.
